DISCOVERY 610, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITION OF THE PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE: THE MOTION IS GRANTED. THE EXISTENCE AND LOCATION OF INFORMATION STORED ON COMPUTERS EITHER IN THE POSSESSION, CUSTODY, OR CONTROL OF DEFENDANTS, THE CHAIN OF CUSTODY OF ANY INFORMATION STORED ON COMPUTERS, THE DOCUMENT RETENTION POLICY AND / OR PRACTICES, AND ANY EFFORTS OR ACTIONS UNDERTAKEN TO PRESERVE INFORMATION RELEVANT TO THE LITIGATION ARE NOT PRIVILEGED. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO A MUTUALLY CONVENIENT TIME TO SCHEDULE THE DEPOSITION. THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT OPPOSED THE INSTANT MOTION WITHOUT SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION AND MADE EVASIVE RESPONSES. MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,762.50 ARE TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN REPORTER: ANTHONY JUDE CORDOVA

Motion To Compel Deposition Testimony Of Deft'S Person Most Knowledgeable
Off Calendar Dec-21-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: MATHEW STEPHENSON, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY, IRINA KHACTOURIAN
VANESSA WHANG, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANTS/OPPOSING PARTIES, IKON OFFICE SOLUTIONS, INC., ET AL.

MATTER ARGUED. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. Plaintiff's motion to compel the deposition of the person most knowledgeable:
The motion is granted. The existence and location of information stored on computers either in the possession, custody, or control of defendants, the chain of custody of any information stored on computers, the document retention policy and / or practices, and any efforts or actions undertaken to preserve information relevant to the litigation are not privileged. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to a mutually convenient time to schedule the deposition

The Court finds that defendant opposed the instant motion without substantial justification and made evasive responses. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $2,762.50 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN REPORTER: ANTHONY JUDE CORDOVA

Motion For Protective Order Or Motion To Quash Subpoenas
Off Calendar Sep-13-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Deborah M. Kochan for plaintiff and moving party: Irina Khactourian
Natalie A. Beccia for defendant and opposing party: Ikon Office Solutions, Inc.

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for a protective order
Denied. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to revising plaintiff's Amended Stipulated Protective Order that is attached as Exhibit H to the motion. Paragraph 2 of the proposed order should include as an additional category of persons entitled to disclosure of information as those persons that counsel for defendants deem necessary in preparation of the defense. The Court does not approve of the requirements set forth in Paragraph 3 as the proposed procedure implicates the attorney work product privilege. Any stipulated protected order should also provide that Counsel for defendant may not disclose or permit to be disclosed to defendants, or anyone else, the names, addresses, or telephone numbers of any person who either prepared the records or is described in the records unless specifically permitted to do so by the court after a hearing and a showing of good cause.

Notwithstanding the above, counsel for defendant may disclose or permit to be disclosed the names, addresses, or telephone numbers of any person described in the records produced pursuant to the subpoenas to persons employed by counsel to assist in the preparation of the case, if that disclosure is required for that preparation. Persons provided this information by counsel shall be informed that further dissemination of the information constitutes a violation of a court order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
